results.editorial:
Mona Elder Care runs a nursing home at 57, Jalan Teratai 2, Taman P. Ramlee, Setapak, on a quiet residential road inside Kuala Lumpur's northern suburbs. The Setapak branch is one of several Mona Elder Care houses in Peninsular Malaysia (others include Pandan Perdana and Taiping). The operator presents itself as a 24-hour eldercare service and accepts both long-stay residents and shorter respite bookings. Google reviews are sparse but positive at 4.8 across 9 ratings as of mid-2026, so prospective families should treat the public sample as small.

The Setapak house lists clinical nursing as its main work rather than purely custodial care. Mona Elder Care advertises support for stroke and coma recovery, post-operative recovery, palliative and terminal care, dementia, and care for adults with disabilities. On the procedure side the company says its nurses handle wound and pressure-sore management, nasogastric (NG) tube changes, urinary catheter care, colostomy stoma care, and tracheostomy maintenance. Day care and short-term respite stays are offered alongside long-term residence. Bed count, room types, nurse-to-resident ratios, doctor visit schedules, and pricing tiers are not published online for this branch.

The Setapak location is a fit for families looking for nurse-led care close to Kuala Lumpur city centre, particularly for residents discharged from hospital with tubes, wounds, or recent strokes. Columbia Asia Setapak and KPJ Tawakkal are nearby for escalation. Because the company runs multiple houses under one brand, ask specifically about the Taman P. Ramlee unit when calling: how many beds, room layouts, the registered nurse roster overnight, monthly fees by room type, what is included versus charged extra (diapers, milk feeds, physio, doctor visits), and the JKM Pusat Jagaan registration number. Photos and a tour are worth requesting before committing.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, respite, dementia, palliative, stroke/coma, post-op
facts.red_flags: Bed count, staffing, pricing not published; small review sample

results.editorial:
EHA Grandview Eldercare Mansion is the Kuala Lumpur branch of EHA ElderCare Group, a Johor-headquartered eldercare operator that says it has been in the sector for over 14 years and runs houses in Kluang, Johor Bahru, and now Kepong/Jalan Ipoh. The KL mansion sits at 6, Lengkungan Vethavanam, Taman Kaya, 51100 KL, between Kepong and Jalan Ipoh, and is positioned to serve the Desa ParkCity, Kepong and Jalan Ipoh catchment. Google ratings are 5.0 across 27 reviews as of mid-2026, which is strong for a relatively new branch. The Chinese trading name 甲洞温馨疗养别墅 signals a Mandarin-speaking operating culture.

EHA's published care menu covers live-in nursing home stays (long and short term), adult day care, and home nursing dispatched from the centre. Group-wide assisted senior living pricing is advertised from RM3,000 per month for the JB and KL houses (RM2,300 in Kluang); the actual room tier and inclusions at the KL branch should be confirmed by phone since prices vary by dependency level and room type. The Taman Kaya house markets itself as offering modern medical equipment, 24/7 healthcare staff coverage, in-house dining tailored to dietary needs, gardens, lounges and activity rooms. Specific bed count, registered nurse roster, doctor visit cadence, and clinical capabilities (PEG, wound, dialysis, oxygen) are not published online.

The KL branch suits families in Kepong, Desa ParkCity, Sri Hartamas, Mont Kiara and Jalan Ipoh who want a Mandarin-friendly nursing home with a group brand behind it and the option to swap between day care, home care and live-in stays as needs change. Worth asking on a visit: registered JKM Pusat Jagaan licence number for this branch (group track record does not transfer to a new house), nurse-to-resident ratio overnight, how dementia residents are managed (locked unit or open layout), halal status of the kitchen, and whether the published RM3,000 figure is for a shared room with basic care or for a private en suite.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, adult day care, assisted living, home nursing
facts.red_flags: Pricing tier vs room type unclear; clinical capabilities not specified for KL branch

results.editorial:
2E Nursing & Healthcare appears on Google Maps as a Kuala Lumpur nursing home with strong but very limited reviews (5.0 across 20 ratings as of mid-2026). It is also listed under categories "service establishment" and "health consultant", which suggests the operator may combine residential elder care with home-nursing and consultancy. There is no live website, public Facebook page, or directory listing on the major Malaysian eldercare aggregators (hati.my, ielder.asia, Mintygreen's KL roundups), so almost no public detail is verifiable.

Families considering this home should call before visiting and ask: exact street address and area, JKM Pusat Jagaan registration number, total beds and current vacancy, room types and monthly fees, whether registered nurses are rostered overnight, doctor visit arrangement, halal kitchen status, languages spoken by carers, and whether they take residents on NG/PEG feeds, with wounds, or with dementia who wander. Request a site visit and photographs of bedrooms, bathrooms, dining and outdoor areas before any deposit.

facts.red_flags: No website, no public profile beyond Google Maps; very thin verifiable footprint

results.editorial:
This listing for Master Care Nursing Homes (Puteri Wangsa) carries a single Google review (5.0) and a Taman Puteri Wangsa address. Taman Puteri Wangsa is in Ulu Tiram, Johor, not Kuala Lumpur, so the state assignment in the directory should be checked. There is a separate Johor-headquartered brand called Master Care Centre (masternursingcare.com) with branches in Bandar JB, Taman Abad and Taman Daya, but its public site does not list a Puteri Wangsa branch, so it is not clear whether this is the same operator or an unrelated home reusing the words "master care".

Because the public footprint is one Google review and no website, families should treat this as unverified. Useful questions on a call: who runs the home and is it part of the Master Care Centre group, JKM Pusat Jagaan licence number, total beds, monthly fees, RN coverage overnight, dementia and PEG capability, halal kitchen, and which hospital they would use in an emergency. Ask for the full address before visiting and verify against the JKM Pusat Jagaan directory.

facts.red_flags: 1 review only; no website; address sits in Johor not KL; may not be linked to the Master Care Centre JB group

results.editorial:
Happy Family Nursing Home (Kepong) is at 335, Jalan Kuang Gunung, Taman Kepong, 52100 Kuala Lumpur. It is one of several houses under the Happy Family brand, which also operates in Taman Kepong (Jalan 2), Taman Desa Jaya, Kajang and Cheras. The official corporate website is currently down, but third-party directory pages confirm the address and phone number 018-777 7383. Google reviews are mixed at 2.8 across 47 ratings as of mid-2026 — a notably low score relative to other Kepong homes and the largest review sample on this batch, so families should weigh recent review themes carefully before booking a visit.

The operator describes itself as a long-running affordable nursing home for the elderly, with bathing, housekeeping, grooming, basic medical support and meals, and says it equips its houses with electronic medical equipment for residents with disabilities or chronic disease. Specific capabilities published online are limited: bed count, registered nurse roster, doctor visit cadence, room types and monthly pricing are not disclosed publicly for this branch, and there is no clear separation in marketing between the Kuang Gunung, Jalan 2, and Desa Jaya houses.

Kepong residents looking for a budget-tier nursing home close to home will recognise the brand, and the multi-house network may help with capacity. Given the 2.8 rating, prospective families should read the actual Google reviews before visiting, ask specifically about staffing ratios overnight, infection-control routines, hygiene and bathing schedules, and how complaints are handled. On a tour: confirm JKM Pusat Jagaan registration number, ask to see the bedrooms in use (not show rooms), check the toilet-to-resident ratio, and ask whether the home takes NG/PEG feeds, wounds and bed-bound residents. Compare against the higher-rated Jalan 2 sister house (4.4) before deciding.

facts.red_flags: Google rating 2.8/5 across 47 reviews; corporate website down; staffing and pricing not published

results.editorial:
OpulenceCARE II (甲洞乐理护老之家) is the second house in the OpulenceCARE chain in Taman Kepong, Kuala Lumpur. The group also runs OpulenceCARE 64 nearby on Jalan Development. Listed Google categories are assisted living facility, nursing home, and senior citizen centre, indicating the home accepts a mix of dependency levels rather than only bed-bound nursing cases. Reviews stand at 3.9 across 8 ratings as of mid-2026 — a small sample, so individual visits matter more than the score. The operator is reachable on +603 6262 9778 and on WhatsApp, and the brand markets in both English and Chinese.

OpulenceCARE describes its houses as offering large rooms, exercise facilities and socialising areas, and positions itself in the cleanliness and environment tier rather than the budget tier. Public detail beyond that is light: bed count for the II house, room types and monthly fees, registered nurse staffing overnight, doctor visit arrangements, dementia handling (whether there is a locked memory unit), and clinical capabilities (PEG, wound, oxygen, dialysis transport) are not published online. The chain has at least two operating houses in Kepong, suggesting a settled local operation.

This branch may suit Kepong-area families wanting a Mandarin-speaking, mid-tier home with shared activity space rather than a hospital-style ward feel. Ask on a visit: which Pusat Jagaan licence number applies to OpulenceCARE II specifically, RN coverage overnight, monthly fees by room type and what is bundled (diapers, milk, physio, doctor), how many residents are on NG/PEG and how many are ambulant, and the home's policy on wandering dementia residents. Compare directly against OpulenceCARE 64 down the road if both have vacancies.

facts.care_types: Assisted living, nursing home, senior centre
facts.red_flags: Small review sample; staffing and pricing not published online

results.slug: 协和开心之家安心养老庭院-甲洞安老院-kepong-nursing-home
results.editorial:
协和开心之家安心养老庭院 (Kepong Nursing Home / 甲洞安老院) sits in Kepong Baru, Kuala Lumpur, on the +60 16-251 5662 line. Google's category for the listing is adult day care centre rather than nursing home, with 5.0 across 2 reviews as of mid-2026, so the public footprint is very thin. There is no live website or active social-media presence for the operator under this Chinese name in standard searches, which makes it hard to verify whether it functions primarily as a day centre, a small residential home, or both.

Families should call before visiting and clarify: is this an adult day care only or does it accept residential stays, total beds and current availability, monthly or daily pricing, JKM Pusat Jagaan registration, RN versus PRA carer staffing, hours of operation, dementia handling, transport to and from home, languages spoken by carers, and halal status of meals if relevant. Visit the premises and inspect bathrooms, bedrooms (if residential), and the activity area before committing.

facts.care_types: Adult day care centre (per Google category); residential status unclear
facts.red_flags: 2 reviews; no website; ambiguity over day-care vs residential

results.editorial:
Happy Family Nursing Home (Taman Kepong), trading also as Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Happy Family, sits at No. 38 & 36, Jalan 2, Taman Kepong, Kuala Lumpur. The business was registered as Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Taman Kepong in June 2015 (registration 002452041A), giving it roughly a decade of trading history at the time of writing. It is a sister house to Happy Family Nursing Home (Kepong) on Jalan Kuang Gunung roughly 600 metres away, and to Happy Family (Desa Jaya). The contact line for this house is 019-777 7328 — different from the Kuang Gunung branch — and Google reviews are 4.4 across 13 ratings as of mid-2026, distinctly higher than the 2.8 score on the sister house.

The Happy Family brand markets itself as an affordable nursing home for elderly residents needing 24-hour care, with bathing, grooming, housekeeping, scheduled meals, basic medical support, and electronic medical equipment for residents with disabilities or chronic conditions. The Jalan 2 location occupies two adjoining shoplots (Nos 36 and 38), which is typical of small KL nursing homes. Specific bed count, registered nurse staffing, doctor visit cadence, and pricing tiers are not published online for this branch, and the central corporate website is currently suspended.

This Jalan 2 branch suits Kepong-area families looking for a budget-tier residential home with a longer trading history and a measurably better Google score than the nearby Kuang Gunung sister house. Worth asking on a visit: which house operates under which JKM Pusat Jagaan licence (the same group can have separate licences), RN overnight, total beds and current vacancy, monthly fees by room type, what is included (diapers, feeds, doctor, physio, transport to dialysis), and whether the home takes residents on NG/PEG feeds, with wounds, or with dementia who wander. Compare in person with the Kuang Gunung house before deciding which Happy Family branch to use.

facts.red_flags: Corporate website suspended; staffing/pricing not published; do not assume sister-house ratings transfer

results.editorial:
House of Glory is a small nursing home at 16, Jalan 23/119, Taman Taynton View, 56000 Cheras, Kuala Lumpur, registered as House Of Glory Sdn. Bhd. (1205244-A). It runs out of a single residential lot in a quiet Cheras neighbourhood, with UKM Specialist Centre and Pantai Hospital Cheras both within roughly a 12-minute drive for hospital escalation. Google reviews are 4.3 across only 3 ratings as of mid-2026, so the public review sample is too small to draw conclusions from — direct site visits matter more than the headline score for a home of this size.

House of Glory's published menu covers long-term residential stay, short-term stay, and day care, in a choice of single, double-shared, or open dormitory rooms. Care described includes scheduled medication administration, home-cooked meals, housekeeping and laundry, family gathering sessions, and a recreational programme covering physical fitness, social and learning activities, plus festival celebrations during Chinese New Year and Christmas (suggesting a primarily Chinese resident profile). The site is bilingual in English and Chinese. Specific bed count, registered nurse coverage, doctor visit arrangements, clinical capabilities (NG/PEG/wound/oxygen) and monthly pricing are not published.

The home is a fit for Cheras-area families wanting a small, home-style residential setting with mixed room tiers, particularly for relatively independent or moderate-dependency elders rather than complex post-acute cases. Worth asking on a tour: JKM Pusat Jagaan registration number, total beds and vacancy in each room type, the registered nurse roster overnight versus daytime carers only, doctor visit arrangement, what monthly fees include (diapers, feeds, doctor, physio, transport), whether the home takes residents on NG/PEG, with active wounds, or with wandering dementia, and which hospital they default to in an emergency.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, day care, short-term stay
facts.red_flags: Only 3 reviews; clinical scope and staffing not published; small operation

results.editorial:
Komune Care is a purpose-built assisted-living and senior daycare facility at 21, Jalan Tasik Permaisuri 2, Bandar Tun Razak, 56000 Kuala Lumpur, operated under UOA Group inside the Komune Living & Wellness mixed-use development overlooking Tasik Permaisuri urban park. It is structurally different from a typical KL shoplot nursing home: of 718 units in the Komune Living tower 141 are dedicated to Komune Care eldercare and 577 operate as serviced hotel-style units, with the design emphasising single-level floors, lift access, wheelchair-wide passageways and intergenerational living. Google reviews are 4.5 across 19 ratings as of mid-2026, and AGECOPE (the Association of Gerontologists, Care Operators and Professionals for the Elderly) lists the centre as a member.

Komune Care's published service set covers assisted living suites, dementia care with structured routines, post-hospital recovery, respite stays, and senior daycare, with capacity quoted as 150 residential beds and a daycare centre that can accommodate over 80 attendees. Residents stay in private en-suite rooms with 24-hour nursing and caregiving cover and emergency response. On-site amenities include a traditional Chinese medicine centre, medical and dental clinics, swimming pools, karaoke, game rooms and a clubhouse. UOA Academy, an HRD Corp-aligned training centre, runs accredited eldercare programmes that feed graduates directly into Komune Care, which is unusual for the local sector and suggests a structured staffing pipeline. Pricing is not published online and is positioned at the premium end of the KL market.

Komune Care suits families looking for a premium, purpose-built setting close to the city for parents who want hotel-grade hospitality with on-site clinical and dementia support, including those recovering from hospital stays. It is generally not the budget-tier shoplot option. Worth asking on a tour: which licence covers the residential beds (Pusat Jagaan or Private Aged Healthcare under Act 802), nurse-to-resident ratio overnight, current monthly rates per room tier and what is bundled versus billed (medications, doctor visits, physio, dietitian), the dementia ward layout and whether it is locked, halal kitchen status, and how the building handles fire evacuation given the tower layout.

facts.care_types: Assisted living, dementia care, senior day care, respite, post-hospital recovery
facts.red_flags: Pricing not published; premium tier; verify which Act the residential licence sits under
